# Konative — Full Reference

> Konative (konative.com) is a vendor-neutral connectivity brokerage — an Avant sub-agent —
> serving Tribal Nations, tribal enterprises, rural broadband authorities, and data-center
> operators across the US and Canada. Suppliers pay Konative's fee when they win business;
> buyers pay nothing. Konative does not write grants and federal money never flows through it.

## Services brokered

Dedicated internet access (DIA), lit and dark fiber, middle-mile and last-mile transport,
SD-WAN and managed network, UCaaS and hosted voice, cloud connectivity (AWS Direct Connect,
Azure ExpressRoute, Google Cloud Interconnect), managed security and SASE, mobility and fixed
wireless, and colocation access — sourced through Avant's portfolio of 100+ suppliers.

Buyer segments: Tribal gaming enterprises, Tribal governments and councils, Tribal economic
development corporations, Tribal healthcare and education, Tribal broadband authorities,
rural enterprises, and data-center operators.

## Tribal Broadband Funding Navigator (/tribal/funding-navigator)

Free resource, no signup. Verified against NTIA primary sources.

On June 17, 2026, NTIA opened two funding opportunities for Tribal Nations. Applications for
both are due September 17, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET via the NTIA grants portal. Awards roll out
on a rolling basis beginning Spring 2027.

### TBCP Round 3 (Tribal Broadband Connectivity Program)
- Total: $540 million. Run by NTIA BroadbandUSA.
- Eligible entities: Tribal governments, Tribal organizations, Tribal colleges and
  universities, the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands, and Alaska Native entities.
- Eligible uses: broadband infrastructure deployment AND broadband use/adoption projects,
  including affordability subsidies (free or reduced-cost service), devices, digital skills,
  telehealth, and distance learning.
- Cost share: no match highlighted in program materials — confirm in the NOFO.

### NEGP (Native Entities Grant Program)
- Total: $250 million, under the Digital Equity Act.
- Awards: $500,000 to $2.5 million each.
- Match: 10%, waivable.
- Purpose: Native entities' digital equity and inclusion activities.

### Context
- TBCP to date: 275 awards, $2.2 billion obligated of the $3 billion program.
- TBCP Round 1 requests exceeded $5 billion — oversubscription is the norm.
- FY2026 E-Rate: Tribal library Category Two funding floor is $66,385 with a 90% maximum
  discount. The FCC's T-LEAP (Tribal Library E-Rate Advocacy Program) offers free help.
- USDA ReConnect currently has no open application window.

### Primary sources
- https://www.ntia.gov/press-release/2026/ntia-announces-two-new-funding-opportunities-expand-broadband-connectivity-tribal-lands
- https://broadbandusa.ntia.gov/sites/default/files/2026-06/NTIA_TBCP3_NOFO.pdf
- https://broadbandusa.ntia.gov/sites/default/files/2026-06/NTIA_NEGP_NOFO_2026.pdf

### Konative's role
Konative doesn't write grants — and the money never flows through Konative. It is a
vendor-neutral connectivity brokerage that helps Tribes scope the network an application
promises, pressure-test carrier costs in the budget, and — post-award — make carriers compete
for the build and the circuits. Grant writers and Native-owned advisors (AMERIND Critical
Infrastructure, Tribal Ready, MuralNet) are partners Konative works alongside, not against.

## Sovereignty Commitment (/tribal/sovereignty)

Six commitments Konative puts in writing for every Tribal engagement:

1. You own the contracts — every carrier agreement is between the carrier and the Tribe or
   tribal enterprise; Konative is agent-of-record working for the Tribe, not the carrier.
2. You own the data — network documentation, quotes, and inventory live in the Tribe's
   systems; Konative keeps copies only with permission; data stays under Tribal jurisdiction.
3. You can walk away anytime — no exclusivity, no lock-in, no termination fees.
4. Transparent economics — suppliers pay Konative a commission, typically 15-20% of monthly
   recurring charges (industry standard); the Tribe pays nothing; commission disclosed on
   any deal upon request.
5. No grant-farming — no percentages of grant awards, no grants written for fees, federal
   money never touches Konative's hands.
6. We show up — in person, at TribalNet and regional convenings, before and after the deal.

Partner ecosystem: Konative works alongside — never against — AMERIND Critical
Infrastructure, Tribal Ready, MuralNet, and tribally-owned telecoms like NTUA (Navajo Tribal
Utility Authority), whose 550+ mile fiber backbone and Shiprock data center show what tribal
ownership builds. They do funding, builds, and advocacy; Konative does multi-carrier
procurement and lifecycle management.

## Site map

- / — Home
- /connectivity — Enterprise connectivity brokerage
- /data-center-connectivity — Data-center connectivity brokerage
- /tribal — Tribal & rural enterprise connectivity (pillar)
- /tribal/funding-navigator — $790M Tribal broadband funding navigator (TBCP R3 + NEGP)
- /tribal/sovereignty — Sovereignty commitment
- /tribal/awards — NTIA TBCP award records
- /map — Interactive infrastructure map
- /markets — Per-state market pages
- /intelligence, /answers, /dispatch — Intelligence and knowledge base
- /call — Book a call (https://cal.com/jeramey-james/15min)
- /contact — Contact form
